data.narrative:
Overland AI is a Series A-stage defense & security company founded by Byron Boots, Greg Okopal, and Stephanie Bonk, having raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $40M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $80M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median of this set is $300M, with a range of $150M–$500M.

Synthesizing across both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $68M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $190M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer multiples drive the upper range. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in how capital deployment and product maturity map to valuation within early-stage defense tech. Key sensitivities include: definition of revenue or technical milestones achieved post-Series A, customer acquisition progress, and whether recent comparable rounds reflect mark-to-market or inflated pricing in the sector.
